video_title: Endlich AAA-Spiele für Leute mit wenig Zeit – perfekt für Steam Deck & Co.
comment_text:
Indiana Jones und 007 sind auch meine Top 2 in diesem Monat. Platz 3 wäre bei mir Forza Horizon 6 geworden, da auch dieser Teil wieder super optimiert für Handhelds ist. Wobei hier zwischen Claw 8Ai+ und SteamDeck was Performance und Bildqualität tatsächlich schon Welten liegen. XESS ist FSR 3.1 einfach mal um längen überlegen.
Übrigens, mit dem Release von Indiana Jones auf der Switch 2, hat Maschine Games auch nochmal einen Patch für die PC Version nachgeschoben, der die CPU Optimierungen der Switch 2 auch auf den PC bringt. Damit lässt sich auch der Ägypten Level, wo vorher in einigen Bereichen gerade mal 24 FPS geschafft wurden, jetzt ohne Probleme mit 30 FPS + auf der Claw spielen.
Bei 007 First Light bin ich sehr auf die Switch 2 Version gespannt. Der unterschied in dem Spiel zwischen FSR und DLSS sind Welten. Daher gehe ich mal davon aus das die Switch Version, ähnlich wie Indiana Jones, eine deutlich bessere Bildschärfe haben wird und damit dann auch wieder die XBox Series S ausstechen wird, was die Bildqualität betrifft.

video_title: Nintendo Switch 2 Review - Best Worst Console Ever.
comment_text: The Switch 2 is my first Nintendo console barely use it but do I regret buying it, surprisingly no. The time I do use it during a home group situation it is amazing, the new controllers are a big improvement over the original. When I play Smash, Mario Kart, or Mario Party with people over it is the best time I have playing a video game. I’ve played some of the single player games for the first time like Odyssey, Wonder, and the new DK, and they are amazing so I don’t regret my time. Marvel Rivals has consumed my life gaming wise, so when it’s added I’ll surely use it more, but for now I’m waiting for the new 3D Mario game or a new group/party type game.

comment_text:
Everything the gabites thought they knew about valve is wrong.  Online and cloud saves are not free.  They're funded by not having a secondhand market. 10 bucks a month in steam sales is 120 bucks a year.  You've already been paying for cloud saves.  Xbox has been charging for online since 2004 and in 25 years they have not turned a dime in profit.  This stuff is not free folks and Valve isn't giving it away because they're "not greedy."

TLDR, valve's books are tighter than we all imagined and they were forced to do this to stay solvent.

Also, price of games being lower on steam is a farce.  RE9 is currently on sale on xbox and it has never gone on sale on steam.  I know because I do not buy digital games day one.  Paying full price to rent a game...no thanks.

You're going to have to build that from scratch with jacked up in price parts if you don't feel safe switching OSs on an existing system. video_title: Nintendo Switch 2 Review - Best Worst Console Ever.
comment_text:
I feel like looking at it as a console is the wrong view; they were never in to compete with PS or Xbox. It is a Gameboy that you can comfortably play on a TV. And that was absolutely genius move by Nintendo.

As a PC gamer with more power than any console will have for a long time, the Switch has been the perfect system: there when I want to pick it up and go and there when I want to melt into my couch. I don’t buy the “pretty” games for it (I frankly don’t buy them much at all; AAA is pretty sh*t these days), I get the niche, usually pixel art, indie stuff, a few Nintendo titles, and retro SNES and Genesis games. There are a few outliers that are a real pleasure on the Switch with my surround system, like Ori.

I did finally just get a proper controller for it (PDP, which should work with the Switch 2 I have coming) and I will say that makes a huge difference. I’m actually finally enjoying BoTW.

My Switch will probably be retired to one of my desk monitors and for out-of-home use until one of my nephews is old enough to not break everything they touch. 😆
